meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no version control (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master User Onboarding: Solving Version Control for Students
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn to implement robust user onboarding and version management using Antigravity and Claude Code in this beginner-friendly tutorial.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your project is a ticking time bomb of lost progress. By integrating an automated user onboarding flow that forces a Git-init sequence upon first launch, you secure your codebase, track progress milestones, and build professional habits that define top-tier software engineers. Start securing your work today.
content.intro:
Imagine the crushing silence of a corrupted file or the panic of a deleted function you can't undo. For a student, the lack of version control isn't just an inconvenience; it is a structural failure that halts your momentum and buries your hard-earned progress in a graveyard of local folders.

But there is a better way. By treating your user onboarding process as the gatekeeper of your development environment, you can bake version control directly into your project's DNA. We are going to transform your workflow, ensuring every line of code is tracked, backed up, and ready for your portfolio.

steps.title: Initialize Repository via Onboarding
steps.description: Trigger a mandatory Git initialization as the first step of your application's setup to ensure no code goes untracked.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate setup script that detects if a .git directory exists and forces git init if not found.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'add a git initialization check to the project entry point'

steps.title: Configure Automated Commit Hooks
steps.description: Create a seamless workflow where the app suggests commits based on the progress made during the onboarding session.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a pre-commit hook script that auto-stages files and prompts the user for a semantic commit message.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'create a .githooks folder and set up a pre-commit script'

steps.title: Establish Remote Origin Connection
steps.description: Link your local project to a remote repository during the onboarding flow to guarantee off-site backups.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a function that asks the user for a GitHub remote URL and executes git remote add origin.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'add a CLI prompt to link the project to a remote GitHub repository'

steps.title: Implement Progress Snapshots
steps.description: Create a 'checkpoint' feature within your onboarding that automates the tagging of successful builds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a CLI command that creates a git tag named 'milestone-v1' when the user completes onboarding.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'add a feature to tag the repository upon successful onboarding completion'

steps.title: Verification and Health Check
steps.description: Add a final diagnostic step that verifies the repository status before the user begins building.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a status check script that displays the current branch and local commit history.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'implement a status check dashboard on app launch'

pro_tips: Always use .gitignore to exclude environment variables; never push your API keys to a public repository.
Adopt Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, docs:) early to make your portfolio look professional to future employers.
Use a README.md as your onboarding dashboard; describe how to run the app, not just what it does.
Automate your branch protection rules early so you don't accidentally push to 'main' without a review.
Commit early and often—small, atomic commits are easier to debug than massive, monolithic changes.
content.next_steps: Once your repository is secured, push your project to GitHub and deploy a live preview link using Vercel or Netlify to share with your peers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the foundation to build secure, professional-grade software that will impress recruiters and mentors alike. Don't let your code disappear into the void—take control of your development journey now. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
